commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Because the first four commandments pertain to God, it is implied that they are contained in the first table. The second table pertains to humans, that they should honor their parents, not murder, not commit adultery, not steal, not bear false witness and covet none of their neighbors’ goods. These six commandments are viewed as written on the second table. .

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: A promise is attached to this commandment…. It is found in the Decalogue. It is the first command in the second table of the law. It was given to the people as they were leaving Egypt. .

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Now, although Marcion has erased (the next clause), "which is the first commandment with promise"
